vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as healthy, living plants. The environment is dominated by dark, potentially waterlogged soil and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected; White Rabbit anchor is present but obscured/degraded.

plant_audit.P3: White Rabbit anchor present, but no Pothos foliage visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, appears saturated or high in organic decay.
biome_observations.surface_debris: Presence of white particulate matter (possibly mineral salts or degraded perlite) and a spoon in the early frames.
biome_observations.lighting_impact: Low-light conditions are hindering visual confirmation of any micro-sprouts.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_T-4: Removal of the spoon from the yellow pot area.
temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-2: Gradual loss of ambient light/contrast; potential drying or settling of soil surface.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_current: Extreme light degradation; the scene is nearly obscured by darkness, rendering further biological assessment impossible without improved illumination.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of photosynthetic activity. The specimens are either dead, buried, or have been removed.
anomalies: Presence of a spoon in early frames (Intervention).
Total absence of expected plant biomass.
Extreme low-light environment preventing accurate botanical identification.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic failure of the indoor botanical setup. Across the five-day sequence, the plants have vanished from view, leaving only the soil and the White Rabbit anchor. The environment has transitioned from a monitored state to one of total darkness and apparent neglect. No living tissue is identifiable in the current frame.
